title: Federal Reserve Board - 2022 Press Releases
summary: This is a listing/index page of Federal Reserve Board press releases from calendar year 2022, containing dozens of announcements spanning monetary policy, banking applications, enforcement actions, regulatory guidance, and institutional announcements. The page is not a single substantive document but rather an archive index of multiple disparate press releases. Published December 22, 2022, this is a historical record approximately 3 years and 3 months old.
key_points: This is an index page listing all Fed press releases from 2022, not a single substantive policy document

Notable market-structure relevant items include: SOFR/LIBOR transition final rule (12/16/2022), FedNow timing update to mid-2023 (8/29/2022), and intraday credit policy updates (12/21/2022 and 12/2/2022)
FOMC meeting minutes released November 23, 2022 (November 1-2 meeting) and October 12, 2022 (September 20-21 meeting) are listed
Discount rate actions noted for multiple Federal Reserve Banks (New York, Philadelphia, Kansas City, Minneapolis, San Francisco)
Contains unrelated content including banking applications, enforcement actions, climate risk proposals, and resolution plan reviews
Source was published 12/22/2022 and represents historical data from calendar year 2022
why_it_matters: This index page aggregates diverse Federal Reserve communications from 2022 but does not itself contain substantive policy details. Individual press releases would need to be accessed separately for actionable information on FOMC decisions, rate guidance, or regulatory frameworks. The listing is too broad to serve as a focused research source for market structure or macro catalysts.
macro_context: The index contains references to FOMC minutes and discount rate actions that would have macro relevance if accessed individually, but the listing page itself does not provide the content needed to assess macro implications.
market_structure_context: Several items in the listing touch on market structure: the SOFR/LIBOR transition rule identifies benchmark replacement rates; FedNow Service update relates to payment infrastructure; intraday credit policy updates affect bank liquidity operations. However, actual details are not present in this index page.
